criteriaResults.criterion: A file named README.md is created
criteriaResults.reasoning: The workspace shows README.md was created and its contents are visible.

criteriaResults.criterion: Contains a project title and description
criteriaResults.reasoning: The README begins with '# quickserve' as the title and provides a one-line description: 'A lightweight, fast HTTP file server written in Go with minimal dependencies and zero configuration needed to get started.'
The Features section lists 8 features including Blazingly Fast, Zero Dependencies, Directory Browsing, CORS Support, Custom Port & Directory, Range Requests, GZIP Compression, and TLS/HTTPS.

criteriaResults.criterion: Includes installation instructions with code blocks
criteriaResults.reasoning: The Quick Start section includes installation via 'go install github.com/example/quickserve@latest' and source build instructions, both wrapped in fenced code blocks.

criteriaResults.criterion: Includes usage examples with code blocks
criteriaResults.reasoning: The Usage Examples section provides multiple examples with fenced bash code blocks, including basic usage, HTTPS, CORS, and custom host examples.
The README includes a License section at the bottom specifying MIT License, though the content appears truncated in the output.

criteriaResults.criterion: Uses proper markdown formatting (headers, lists, code fences)
criteriaResults.reasoning: The README uses proper markdown including H1/H2/H3 headers, bullet lists with bold text, fenced code blocks with language tags, and a formatted table for configuration options.
